title: dysoxylum macrocarpum
intro: Dysoxylum macrocarpum is a species of flowering plant in the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the Indian satinwood or satinwood. It is a large evergreen tree native to the Western Ghats of India and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which contribute to its various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating wounds. The herbal preparations of Dysoxylum macrocarpum include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, and its wood is also used in woodworking due to its durability and resistance to insects.
